question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged user submit a RecordItem with a getTransactionFee() that is non-zero but the transaction status is SUCCESS with no corresponding debit in the transfer list, causing chargedTxFee to be stored as non-zero while no actual fee transfer is recorded, creating an inconsistency in fee accounting?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RecordItemAggregator.accept()] Can an unprivileged user submit a contract call where the ContractFunctionResult has a getBloom() value that, when OR'd into the logsBloom via logsBloom.or(DomainUtils.toBytes(result.getBloom())), produces a bloom filter that falsely indicates the presence of fee-related log events, causing incorrect fee event detection?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged user cause a RecordFile to be processed where the getItems() stream contains a RecordItem with a null TransactionRecord, causing a NullPointerException in recordItemListener.onItem() that triggers the @Retryable mechanism, repeatedly attempting to process the same fee record and causing resource exhaustion?
